swatches.note: Brief warm cast in the air before the sun fully rises.

swatches.name: Swamp water tannin, black tea
swatches.note: The deeply tea-stained colour of cypress-leaf decomposition water.

swatches.name: Egret white, wading bird
swatches.note: Ardea alba standing in the shallows — the only true white in the slough.

d.text: Cypress-knee grey across the entire water surface.

s.text: Spanish moss, mud-brown, dawn pink — the working surrounds.

a.text: Tannin water and egret white — the two extremes of contrast.

palettes.refuses: Neon-bayou tourism magenta. The slough is grey, brown, and silver with bird-white; everything else is Bourbon Street, not the basin.

palettes.kicker: 55° N · July · 13:00 · Lowland Kamchatkan taiga in heavy mosquito season, near the Avacha river
palettes.title: Birch leaf and fireweed in full summer, the volcanic peaks visible only as silhouettes through overcast
palettes.source: Color from the working summer territory of the Russian Far East: birch-leaf green, fireweed pink, bear-fern groundcover, weathered volcanic ash, salmon stream, summer overcast.

swatches.name: Birch leaf, full summer
swatches.note: Betula ermanii at midsummer; the dominant deciduous green at this latitude.

swatches.name: Low-cloud grey, summer overcast
swatches.note: The standard Kamchatkan summer sky — never deep blue at this latitude.

swatches.name: Bear-fern green, ground cover
swatches.note: Dryopteris at thigh height across most of the taiga floor.

swatches.name: Salmon stream, glacial
swatches.note: Cold spawning river cutting through the lowland.

swatches.name: Fireweed pink, willow-herb spike
swatches.note: Chamaenerion angustifolium in full bloom across burn-recovery ground.

swatches.name: Volcanic ash, weathered
swatches.note: Deposits from previous Avacha eruptions, mixed into the trail soil.

d.text: Birch-leaf summer green carries the territory.

s.text: Overcast cloud, bear-fern, salmon stream — the surrounds.

a.text: Fireweed pink and volcanic ash, in small reads only.

palettes.refuses: Marketed Kamchatka glacier-teal. The lowland taiga in July is green and grey; the volcanic-peak teal palette belongs to the helicopter brochure.

volumes.title: Off-Season & Weather
volumes.eyebrow: Field Notebook № 04 · Travel · Vol XII · Generative Palette Study
volumes.h1: Four palettes from the wrong week to be there.
preface: Every territory has a wrong week — the season the brochures don't show. Plum-rain in a Chinese tea region. Latvian winter. Typhoon afternoon on Okinawa. July fog at Point Reyes. The palettes that show up in those weeks are the territory's truer ones, because nobody is curating them.
Each refuses the in-season postcard: spring cherry blossom, Baltic-summer blue, Okinawa-resort turquoise, California-poster sunshine. The dominant is the weather the photographer skipped.

palettes.kicker: 31° N · June · 15:00 · Yixing teapot district during the plum-rain season
palettes.title: The hour the rain pauses, and the bamboo grove behind the kiln is steaming
palettes.source: Color from a southern-Chinese teapot-making town in méiyǔ (plum-rain) season: zisha clay red, wet-stone path, bamboo grove green in steady rain, monsoon sky white, the grey kiln-fired rim of an unfinished teapot, the river-haze of Taihu in the distance.

swatches.name: Monsoon sky white, plum-rain
swatches.note: Continuous overcast of mid-June southern China; the dominant ambient.

swatches.name: Wet stone path, slate
swatches.note: Kiln-yard paving wet from continuous rain.

swatches.name: Bamboo wet green, grove
swatches.note: Phyllostachys grove behind the workshop, deepened by rain.

swatches.name: River haze, Taihu
swatches.note: Lake fog rolled inland by the season.

swatches.name: Zisha clay, fired teapot red
swatches.note: Famous Yixing teapot clay, the deep oxide red of the local mineral.

swatches.name: Fired clay rim, kiln-mark grey
swatches.note: Unglazed rim of an unfinished pot, kiln-fired but uncured.

d.text: Plum-rain sky carries everything; even the workshops read through it.

s.text: Wet stone, bamboo, river haze — the rain-soaked surrounds.

a.text: Zisha red and kiln-fired rim, in small fired reads only.

palettes.refuses: Cherry-blossom pink. The plum-rain regions of southern China use deep tea-bowl reds and bamboo greens; pink is the wrong season and the wrong nation.

palettes.kicker: 57° N · January · 12:00 · Jūrmala beachfront, Latvia, snow on the pier and ice on the Gulf
palettes.title: The Baltic at the deepest cold of the year, with the dacha shutters mint-green against the snow and the kvass cart still out
palettes.source: Color from a Latvian seaside town in winter: snow on pine, the Baltic Sea grey-blue with floating brash ice, painted dacha mint-green shutters, sauna-bench birch, kvass amber on the cart, lantern yellow at the pier head.

swatches.name: Snow on pine bough
swatches.note: Loaded conifer along the boardwalk; reads pure cold-white at noon.

swatches.name: Baltic Sea grey-blue, ice-edged
swatches.note: The Gulf of Riga in January — slate water with floating brash ice.

swatches.name: Sauna birch, fresh-cut bench
swatches.note: Betula pendula bench in a public sauna; the regional sit-surface.

swatches.name: Dacha mint-green shutter
swatches.note: Painted summer-house shutter visible from the boardwalk all year.

swatches.name: Kvass amber, glass
swatches.note: Fermented-bread drink from the still-open cart at the pier entrance.

swatches.name: Lantern yellow, pier light
swatches.note: Sodium pier-head lamp at the boardwalk's seaward end.

d.text: Snow on pine carries the boardwalk and the dacha gardens.

s.text: Baltic grey, sauna birch, dacha mint-green — the working surrounds.

a.text: Kvass amber and lantern yellow — the only saturated reads in the territory.

palettes.refuses: Baltic-summer-postcard blue-and-yellow. Jūrmala in January is grey, mint, and birch; the saturated summer palette is six months away.

palettes.kicker: 26° N · September · 14:30 · Okinawa coast under a category-2 typhoon, mid-afternoon
palettes.title: The hour the eye-wall is two hundred kilometres out and the shutters are nailed, with the banyan whipping flat
palettes.source: Color from a Ryukyuan coast in active typhoon weather: typhoon-cloud green-grey, wet asphalt, banyan canopy whipped dark, Ryukyuan tile-red on a roof not yet stripped, sugarcane field pre-harvest, fallen plumeria blossom on the wet road.

swatches.name: Typhoon cloud green-grey
swatches.note: The standard cloud colour during an active western-Pacific typhoon afternoon.

swatches.name: Wet asphalt, road glare
swatches.note: Saturated road surface with horizontal rain running across it.

swatches.name: Banyan dark canopy, wind-tossed
swatches.note: Ficus microcarpa windbreak, leaves storm-darkened.

swatches.name: Sugarcane field, pre-harvest
swatches.note: Saccharum officinarum fields visible inland when wind drops.

swatches.name: Ryukyuan tile-red, roof
swatches.note: Traditional akagawara roof tile; brilliant in this weather against the grey.

swatches.name: Plumeria washed pale, fallen blossom
swatches.note: Plumeria blossoms knocked down by the wind onto wet pavement.

d.text: Typhoon cloud carries every visible square metre of sky.

s.text: Wet asphalt, banyan dark, sugarcane field — the wind-blown surrounds.

a.text: Ryukyuan tile-red and plumeria pale — the only warm reads in the storm.

palettes.refuses: Tropical-resort turquoise. Okinawa in a typhoon is grey-green and dangerous; the brochure blue is on hold.

palettes.kicker: 38° N · July · 11:00 · Point Reyes peninsula, California, the marine layer locked in for the third week
palettes.title: Coastal fog all the way to Tomales Bay, with the cypress wind-shaped and the abalone shell glinting at the wrack line
palettes.source: Color from the Northern California coast in its summer fog month: coastal-fog white, salt-tolerant beach grass, weather-grey cypress, abalone-shell iridescence, kelp wrack on the strandline, the warm sandstone of the bluffs.

swatches.name: Coastal fog white
swatches.note: The marine layer locked along the peninsula for weeks in July — the dominant for the month.

swatches.name: Beach grass, salt-tolerant green
swatches.note: Ammophila on the foredunes — the territory's only consistent green.

swatches.name: Weather-grey cypress, wind-shaped
swatches.note: Hesperocyparis macrocarpa shaped by the prevailing wind; the structural tree.

swatches.name: Sandstone bluff, low light
swatches.note: Tomales-Bay-side cliff sandstone seen through fog.

swatches.name: Abalone shell, iridescent inner
swatches.note: Haliotis inner-shell glint at the wrack line — the brief saturated read.

swatches.name: Kelp wrack brown, strandline
swatches.note: Macrocystis washed up overnight, dark and warm against the fog grey.

d.text: Coastal fog is the dominant for the entire month of July.

s.text: Beach grass, weather-grey cypress, sandstone bluff — the surrounds in fog.

a.text: Abalone shell and kelp wrack — the only reads with any saturation.

palettes.refuses: California-poster sunshine. Point Reyes in July is the fog that ate San Francisco; the brand-California yellow is two hundred miles south.
